mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and yields negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When real personal consumption expenditures rise while the consumer price index declines, disposable income expands and corporate profit margins improve, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >55% of occurrences, or the interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which signals robust economic activity and higher near-term corporate cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day returns in >70% of cases, or the signal reverses direction during periods of supply chain bottlenecks.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, borrowing costs for corporations and households become restrictive enough to suppress capital expenditure and consumer debt, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in >60% of periods above the threshold, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance when inflation expectations are anchored below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, pushing S&P 500 returns positive over a 60-day horizon as earnings forecasts rise.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >50% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when GDP growth falls below 1.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate increased construction activity and consumer confidence in long-term wealth, which correlates with higher corporate investment and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 20-day returns in >65% of observations, or the correlation coefficient remains below 0.05 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing output and economic momentum, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in >60% of triggered cases, or market participants front-run the data release eliminating the post-release return effect.
